title: [FIRRTL] How does Analog type work with instances?
body:
Analog types are implicitly "inout" sort of things that can only be casted to integer and clock when they are used as an input.  

A few questions:

1) Can they be used as output ports?  If so, are they of type `flip<Analog<42>>` or just `Analog<42>`?
2) When dealing with an instance of a module that takes Analog inputs, do we get a bundle result with a flipped analog type?

@seldridge do you know how this works?
